condition_precedents: Demand-based scheduling system (e.g. Kronos, NICE WFM) deployed
Labour standards (tasks per hour) established and validated
Store manager training on scheduling optimisation completed
industry_sources: RAI — India Retail Report 2024 (rai.net.in/) — store labour productivity benchmarks (sales per FTE)
McKinsey & Company — Reimagining the Store (mckinsey.com/industries/retail) — labour scheduling optimisation 8-18%
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

condition_precedents: Category profitability analysis completed (GMROI per category)
Joint business planning (JBP) framework established with top 20 vendors
Vendor trade funding terms benchmarked and renegotiated

Nielsen — Retail Execution Excellence 2024 (nielsen.com/insights/) — category management ROI

Lease break or non-renewal strategy agreed for underperforming stores

source_verification_note: industry_sources entries indicate the published works that directionally support each lever's savings thesis. Where no external benchmark was found, INTERNAL_CALIBRATION is noted. All cited sources are real publications; specific savings percentages remain internal calibrations unless the source directly cites a matching range.
